description_long: This is a classic wooden barrel with a rounded, bulging midsection and flat, circular top and bottom. The barrel is constructed from vertical wooden staves, each displaying a natural wood grain texture, and is reinforced by four evenly spaced, metallic hoops that encircle its body. The top surface is made of wooden planks fitted tightly together, and the overall finish is matte with visible wood grain and subtle imperfections. One side of the barrel features a small, round bunghole, while another side displays white, hand-painted text that appears to read 'Glarg's.' The wood is a warm brown, and the metal hoops are a muted gray-blue, giving the barrel a sturdy, utilitarian appearance. This type of barrel is commonly found in urban environments such as alleys, storage yards, or near taverns and markets.
description: A sturdy wooden barrel with metal hoops, visible wood grain, and hand-painted white text on one side. The barrel has a classic bulging shape and a small bunghole.
description_view_0: This view shows the barrel's front with a small bunghole visible on the lower midsection and the wood grain clearly defined.
description_view_1: This side view highlights the uninterrupted wood grain and metal hoops, with no visible markings or bunghole.
description_view_2: This view displays the hand-painted white text 'Glarg's' on the barrel's side, along with the metal hoops and wood texture.
description_view_3: This angle shows the opposite side of the barrel, with a partial view of the white text and no bunghole visible.
